seoTitle: How to Add Dynamic Text and DAX-Driven Titles in Power BI
metaDescription: Learn how to add dynamic, DAX-driven text and titles in Power BI so report headings automatically update based on filters and current data.
intro: A static title can quickly feel out of date once filters change or new data comes in, which is where dynamic, DAX-driven text comes in. This guide explains how to build report titles and labels that update automatically based on your data. It's a useful technique for anyone who wants reports to feel more responsive and less like fixed documents.

steps.heading: Understand DAX-driven titles
steps.body: Instead of typing static text into a title, a DAX-driven title uses a measure that returns text, which can then reflect the current filter selection or data state.

steps.heading: Build the DAX measure for your text
steps.body: Write a measure using functions like SELECTEDVALUE or CONCATENATE to construct the dynamic string you want to display, based on relevant fields.

steps.heading: Implement the dynamic text in your report
steps.body: Apply the measure to a title, text box, or card's conditional formatting for the field value so it renders your DAX-generated text on the canvas.

steps.heading: Explore use cases for dynamic text
steps.body: Consider scenarios like a title that shows the currently selected region and date range, so viewers always know exactly what they're looking at.

steps.heading: Apply best practices
steps.body: Keep the underlying DAX simple where possible and test the dynamic text with different filter combinations to make sure it always renders sensibly.

faq.question: Can regular text boxes use DAX-driven titles, or only visual titles?
faq.answer: Standard text boxes can display dynamic text if you insert a data field into them, while visual titles can be set to reference a measure directly through conditional formatting.

faq.question: What happens if my DAX title measure returns multiple values?
faq.answer: Functions like SELECTEDVALUE return blank or a specified default when multiple values are in context, so it's worth planning for that with fallback text.

faq.question: Do dynamic titles slow down report performance?
faq.answer: A well-written measure for dynamic text typically has minimal performance impact, though overly complex logic could add some overhead.

faq.question: Can I combine multiple fields into one dynamic title?
faq.answer: Yes, using DAX text functions you can concatenate multiple fields and static text together into a single dynamic string.
